channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: "I Am Out": Tucker Carlson Says He Won't Support Republican Party
item.description: Tucker Carlson supported Trump in 2024. After the war began, he apologised for supporting the then-presidential candidate and "misleading people," saying it wasn't intentional.
item.content:encoded: Tucker Carlson supported Trump in 2024. After the war began, he apologised for supporting the then-presidential candidate and "misleading people," saying it wasn't intentional.

item.title: Shooting At Canadian Jewish Sites Leaves 3 Dead, Including Gunman: Cops

media:content.media:description: They separately announced that a suspect and resident had been killed.

item.description: A shooting Monday in a Jewish neighborhood of Montreal left three people dead, including a police officer, a resident, and the alleged assailant, police in the Canadian city said.
item.content:encoded: A shooting Monday in a Jewish neighborhood of Montreal left three people dead, including a police officer, a resident, and the alleged assailant, police in the Canadian city said.

item.title: Pentagon Seeks $80 Billion From Congress For Iran War Amid Peace Talks

media:content.media:description: The Pentagon has told senators it needs roughly $80 billion.

item.description: The Pentagon has told senators it needs roughly $80 billion, mostly to cover the cost of the US war against Iran, adding to what is already a sizable military spending boost being sought by President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded: The Pentagon has told senators it needs roughly $80 billion, mostly to cover the cost of the US war against Iran, adding to what is already a sizable military spending boost being sought by President Donald Trump.

item.title: Trump Threatens Prison For Damage To Washington Reflecting Pool

media:content.media:description: Trump said that there is a 10 year prison sentence for the destruction.

item.description: The president has launched a series of projects around Washington to put his stamp on the city, the main one being his expensive plan to build a huge ballroom at the White House.
item.content:encoded: The president has launched a series of projects around Washington to put his stamp on the city, the main one being his expensive plan to build a huge ballroom at the White House.

item.title: Iran Rejects JD Vance's Claims Of Tehran Allowing Nuclear Inspectors

media:content.media:description: Iran said that interaction with the UN nuclear watchdog will be based on the "Safeguards Agreements".

item.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson responded to Vance's remarks, stating that the interaction with the United Nations nuclear watchdog will be based on the "Safeguards Agreements" between Tehran and the IAEA.
item.content:encoded: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson responded to Vance's remarks, stating that the interaction with the United Nations nuclear watchdog will be based on the "Safeguards Agreements" between Tehran and the IAEA.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Says North Korea To Exercise Its Position As Nuclear State
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said exercising the country's position as a nuclear weapons state is the only way to cope with an unpredictable and complicated global security situation, KCNA state news agency reported on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said exercising the country's position as a nuclear weapons state is the only way to cope with an unpredictable and complicated global security situation, KCNA state news agency reported on Tuesday.

item.title: "I Will Do What I Have To Do" If Iran Does Not Stick To Deal, Warns Trump

media:content.media:description: Trump said Iran was supposed to use the money being unfrozen to buy food exclusively from the US.

item.description: Iran War: Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed an interim US-Iran deal last week, more than three months after the US and Israel attacked Iran and Iran responded with its own attacks on Israel and Gulf states with US bases.
item.content:encoded: Iran War: Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed an interim US-Iran deal last week, more than three months after the US and Israel attacked Iran and Iran responded with its own attacks on Israel and Gulf states with US bases.

item.title: 36-Year-Old Man Charged In Scotland With Anti-Muslim Attacks In Court

media:content.media:description: A 36-year-old Scottish man appeared in court Tuesday charged with five counts of attempted murder.

item.description: A 36-year-old Scottish man appeared in court Tuesday charged with five counts of attempted murder with a racist and terror motive after an attack left five people injured last Friday.
item.content:encoded: A 36-year-old Scottish man appeared in court Tuesday charged with five counts of attempted murder with a racist and terror motive after an attack left five people injured last Friday.

item.title: Delhi Police Files FIR After Social Media Influencer Alleges Rape

media:content.media:description: The woman alleged that man called her to a hotel in Burari in September 2022 and sexually assaulted her.

item.description: Based on the woman's complaint, an FIR was registered at Burari Police Station on June 10, and an investigation is underway, a senior police officer said.
item.content:encoded: Based on the woman's complaint, an FIR was registered at Burari Police Station on June 10, and an investigation is underway, a senior police officer said.

item.title: Honduras Planning To Buy Ukrainian Drones To Fight Drug Trade

media:content.media:description: Honduras President said that country is buyig Ukrainian drones to protect border.

item.description: Honduras is planning to buy Ukrainian drones for border policing and anti-drug operations, President Nasry Asfura told AFP on Monday.
item.content:encoded: Honduras is planning to buy Ukrainian drones for border policing and anti-drug operations, President Nasry Asfura told AFP on Monday.

item.title: $300-Billion Fund Sits At Heart Of US-Iran Deal: Who Pays, What To Know

media:content.media:description: US hawks have lambasted the Trump administration for giving too many concessions to Iran.

item.description: The memorandum of understanding says Washington "undertakes with regional partners to develop a definitive mutually agreed plan with at least $300 billion for the reconstruction and economic development" of Iran.
item.content:encoded: The memorandum of understanding says Washington "undertakes with regional partners to develop a definitive mutually agreed plan with at least $300 billion for the reconstruction and economic development" of Iran.

item.title: "Months, Not Years": Five Eyes Alliance's Big Warning On AI Preparedness

media:content.media:description: The alliance said cyber resilience had become essential for businesses.

item.description: The warning came in a joint statement from the Five Eyes Intelligence Oversight and Review Council (FIORC), a grouping of intelligence and security entities from Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the United Kingdom and the United States.
item.content:encoded: The warning came in a joint statement from the Five Eyes Intelligence Oversight and Review Council (FIORC), a grouping of intelligence and security entities from Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the United Kingdom and the United States.

item.title: Mahrang Baloch, Prominent Baloch Activist, Sent To Life Imprisonment By Pakistan

media:content.media:description: The sentence has attracted widespread criticism.

item.description: Currently in custody, Mahrang Balcoh has been for years a vocal critic of the policies of the Pakistani government toward the Baloch People.
item.content:encoded: Currently in custody, Mahrang Balcoh has been for years a vocal critic of the policies of the Pakistani government toward the Baloch People.

item.title: 2 Children Found Dead In Car In France As Europe Sizzles Under Heatwave

media:content.media:description: Two children were found dead in a car in France on Monday.

item.description: The children, aged two and four, were found in their family car in a residential parking lot in Carpentras, where investigators said they believed the heatwave was the most likely reason for the deaths.
item.content:encoded: The children, aged two and four, were found in their family car in a residential parking lot in Carpentras, where investigators said they believed the heatwave was the most likely reason for the deaths.

item.title: Damage To Buildings Estimated At $1.38 Billion In Latest Israel-Lebanon War

media:content.media:description: Benjamin Netanyahu said on Sunday that Israeli forces would remain in southern Lebanon

item.description: Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East conflict on March 2 by launching rocket fire at Israel in support of its backer Iran.
item.content:encoded: Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East conflict on March 2 by launching rocket fire at Israel in support of its backer Iran.

item.title: Iran Says Strait Of Hormuz Closed, Tracking Data Shows Ships Crossing

media:content.media:description: Traffic on Saturday hit the highest levels since the start of the war.

item.description: As of 1530 GMT on Monday (late afternoon local time), the Kpler maritime tracking platform had already recorded 26 transits by commodity ships.
item.content:encoded: As of 1530 GMT on Monday (late afternoon local time), the Kpler maritime tracking platform had already recorded 26 transits by commodity ships.

item.title: US To Ensure Any Unfrozen Iranian Funds Won't Finance Terrorism: JD Vance

media:content.media:description: The talks were the first stage of a two-month negotiating period.

item.description: Iran has been subject to asset freezes and sweeping sanctions by the United States and other Western countries since its 1979 Islamic revolution that toppled the US-backed Shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi.
item.content:encoded: Iran has been subject to asset freezes and sweeping sanctions by the United States and other Western countries since its 1979 Islamic revolution that toppled the US-backed Shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi.

item.title: Analysis: 7 PMs In 10 Years - Understanding Britain's Political Crisis

media:content.media:description: Since 2016, the United Kingdom has witnessed an unusually high turnover of prime ministers.

item.description: A large majority in Parliament often suggests political stability. But Britain's recent experience shows that numbers alone do not guarantee strong public support.
item.content:encoded: A large majority in Parliament often suggests political stability. But Britain's recent experience shows that numbers alone do not guarantee strong public support.

item.title: 12 Indians Killed In Qatar Gas Plant Explosion, Says Embassy

media:content.media:description: A huge blast had rocked Qatar on Sunday.

item.description: The blast happened as workers were restarting operations, which were halted after an Iranian attack in March.
item.content:encoded: The blast happened as workers were restarting operations, which were halted after an Iranian attack in March.

item.title: Explained: How Next UK Prime Minister Will Be Chosen As Keir Starmer Resigns

media:content.media:description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Monday he would resign.

item.description: Any candidate wishing to replace Starmer would need to secure the support of 20 per cent of Labour members of parliament.
item.content:encoded: Any candidate wishing to replace Starmer would need to secure the support of 20 per cent of Labour members of parliament.
